data.title: Is Estee Lauder (EL) a Good Stock to Buy?
data.description:
Estee Lauder's (EL) growth prospects depend on its new "Beauty Reimagined" strategy, which aims to restore sustainable sales growth. The company is focused on cost efficiencies and supply‑chain improvements to rebuild margins. Also, management expects these moves, along with geographic expansion in markets like India and the Middle East, to support a return to growth over the next few years. Currently, EL stock is a Strong Buy, according to the technical indicators, implying further upside from current levels.

data.title: QSR, MCD, CMG: A Total of 4,000 Restaurants in Canada Forecast to Shutdown in 2026
data.description:
Things appear to be going from bad to worse with Canada's economy, impacting the food services industry.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Persistent inflation, rising food prices, and U.S. tariffs are conspiring to slow Canada's economy and clobber the country's restaurant industry. A new study has found that about 7,000 restaurants, diners, and pubs shut down in 2025, and another 4,000 are forecast to close their doors this year.

The study by Dalhousie University in Halifax, Nova Scotia says that Canadian consumers are pulling back on discretionary spending and eating out less often. At the same time, Canadians are becoming pickier about where they eat and more frugal with their food budgets.

Implications for Restaurants in Canada

While the majority of restaurants shutting down are independently owned, the situation in Canada has implications for publicly traded restaurant chains that operate in the country. From homegrown companies such as Restaurant Brands International (QSR), which owns the Burger King hamburger and Popeyes chicken chains, to Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG), which is expanding across Canada.

Canada is also a major market for restaurant chains such as McDonald's (MCD) and Starbucks (SBUX). In fact, Starbucks announced last fall that it is closing 50 locations throughout Canada, mostly in the major cities of Toronto and Vancouver, due to slumping sales and chronic underperformance.

News of the widespread restaurant closures comes as Canada's unemployment rate rose 0.3 percentage points to 6.8% in December as the country's labor market deteriorates and more people look for work.

Is QSR Stock a Buy?

Restaurant Brands International's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy, eight Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average QSR price target of $76.57 implies 12.50% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Chinese Humanoid Robots Leaving U.S. Rivals Tesla and Nvidia in the Dust
data.description:
China is winning the humanoid robot wars striding past their U.S. rivals such as Tesla (TSLA) and Nvidia (NVDA) in shipments, price and sales.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to new data from Omdia, Chinese humanoid robot makers dominated the market in 2025, with five firms in the top 10, and it could be the same pattern in 2026.

Thousands of Humanoids

Market research firm Omdia found that start-up Shanghai AgiBot InnovationTechnology was top of the robot class shipping 5,168 humanoids last year. That amounts to around 40% of the overall market.

Unitree Robotics, whose models are well-versed in winning sporting medals for their athleticism, sprinted to second spot with 4,200 humanoids. That accounted for a third of the overall market. UBTech was third with annual shipments of 1,000 units.

In its report, General-Purpose Embodied Robotics Market Radar, Omdia said the global humanoid robot market entered a phase of rapid growth in 2025, with total shipments expected to reach about 13,000 units for the year.

Humanoid robots are built and designed to look like and move like human beings. They are powered by AI algorithms and complex hardware like semiconductors. They could be used in a whole range of settings from the battlefield, to the factory floor, in hospital surgeries or dusting away the cobwebs in your living room.

U.S. Robots Yet to Fire

Global sales more than quintupled from 2024, the figures said, highlighting interest in what some refer to as "physical AI", as well as the ability of Chinese companies to produce such robots at industrial scale.

"Chinese vendors are setting benchmarks in large-scale production," Omdia said.

U.S firms are still very much in the early stage of development such as Tesla's Optimus, which is set to begin mass production in 2026. Last year, Nvidia launched the Isaac GR00T N1, the world's first open, fully customizable foundation model for generalized humanoid reasoning and skills. It can grasp, move objects with one or both arms, and transfer items from one arm to another.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: S&P 500 Advances on Optimistic Consumer Sentiment Data
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading at an intraday high on Friday following a better-than-expected consumer sentiment report from the University of Michigan.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

January's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment came in at 54, above the consensus estimate of 53.5 and rising from 52.9 in December. The index has now risen for two consecutive months and is at its highest level since September 2025.

"Although consumers' worries about tariffs appear to be gradually receding, they remain guarded about the overall strength of business conditions and labor markets," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

Inflation and Labor Market Fears Remain Worrying for Consumers

Hsu added that consumers continue to be concerned about rising prices and a weakening labor market. This morning, the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that December's unemployment rate fell to 4.4% from 4.5% during the prior month, while nonfarm payrolls increased by 50,000, below the estimate of 70,000.

Furthermore, year-ahead inflation expectations remained steady at 4.2%, which is the lowest since January 2025, when it stood at 3.3%. At the same time, long-run inflation expectations rose by 0.2% to 3.4% and are now above the 2025 range of 2.8% to 3.2%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: BNP Paribas Stock's New Forecast Smashes through Market Doubt as 3 Financial Giants Sound the Bull Horn
data.description:
The tide is finally turning for France's largest lender. After a year of being overlooked, BNP Paribas (FR:BNP) is seeing a massive surge in confidence from the world's biggest investment firms. JPMorgan (JPM) analyst Delphine Lee led the charge on Friday, upgrading the bank and calling it the "least expensive" large bank in Europe. This follows similar "buy" moves from UBS analyst Jason Napier and Redburn earlier in the week. Investors are clearly listening, as the stock jumped more than 4% this week alone, making it the best performer in the entire banking sector.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BNP's Finances Are Looking Much Better

The main reason for this sudden change in heart is that the bank's finances are looking much healthier. JPMorgan analysts noted that the "capital concerns" that used to scare away investors are no longer a problem. Driving this shift is a clear improvement in how much profit the bank is making from its day-to-day operations. At the heart of the new strategy lies a plan to sell off assets in Turkey and Algeria, which will give the bank a cash cushion and help it hit its ambitious revenue targets. With the stock trading at just 7.5 times its expected earnings, many believe it is too cheap to ignore.

Legal Clouds Part for a Blue Sky Scenario

While the bank has been stuck in a long and messy court battle in the U.S. regarding past business in Sudan, the outlook is finally getting brighter. Just this week, a judge cleared the path for BNP Paribas to appeal a previous verdict. The bank is using its recent asset sales to build a fortress that can easily cover any remaining legal costs. Analysts at JPMorgan are so confident that they have mapped out a "Blue Sky" plan. If the bank wins its appeal and keeps its revenue growing, the stock price could potentially jump by 40% from where it sits today.

Investors Sprint Toward the Value Trap Exit

European banks had a great 2025, but BNP Paribas actually lagged behind the rest of the market. Now, the bank is playing catch-up. UBS analysts have added the stock to their "top picks" list, raising their price target to €103. This suggests that even without a perfect "blue sky" outcome, there is still plenty of room for the stock to grow. As the era of high interest rates continues to pad the bank's bottom line, the investors who bought in early are looking at what could be the biggest banking comeback of 2026.

Is BNP Paribas Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, BNP stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on five Buys and five Hold ratings assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month BNP price target sits at €89, implying a 7.9% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: From Humans to Bots: How Range Is Using AI to Crack the $139 Trillion Wealth Puzzle
data.description:
Five years ago, investor Fahad Hassan and software engineer David Cusatis teamed up for one simple task: disrupt the global wealth management industry with artificial intelligence. In October, they launched Rai, a personalized AI wealth advisor, to keep the dream alive.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A Trillion-Dollar Market and the American Nightmare

The global wealth management industry represents a massive opportunity, as asset and wealth managers collectively managed about $139 trillion in 2024, according to PricewaterhouseCoopers. However, the majority of Americans avoid financial advisors due to steep account minimums, percentage-based fees, and an industry geared toward the ultra-wealthy.

And this is where Hassan's Range comes in. Range is an AI-focused wealth management startup with one simple goal: replace the hundreds of thousands of human financial advisors scattered around the world with AI-powered advisors starting with the U.S. 

Range's aim is backed by several private equity firms, including Gradient Venture, the AI-focused venture arm of Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google. In November,  the startup raised $60 million from its Series C funding round, led by Scale Venture Partners. 

The Cost Problem

In the U.S. alone, hundreds of financial firms – from startups to legacy institutions – leverage AI tools to augment human advisors or power robo-advisory platforms. To disrupt this crowded market, a company needs a truly distinctive edge. Range separates itself by placing its AI wealth advisor front and center, making it the core member experience rather than a background utility.

American investors usually pay about 1% of their managed assets in management fees, especially for accounts under $1 million, according to AdvisoryHQ. The cost can quickly reach up to over 2% with the addition of platform, transaction, and other types of charges. Robo-advisors usually charge less. 

According to research firm Cerulli Associates, the total financial wealth of all U.S. households grew by 16% to over $90 trillion in 2024. Yet, only 27% of Americans use financial advisors, according to a 2024 survey by public opinion and data company, YouGov. Other analysts have even suggested that the rate could be significantly lower.

Range Bets on AI, Affordable Fees

Range is turning  things around by deploying a hybrid human advisor plus AI-powered model coupled with no AUM fees1 or account minimums. In October, the startup rolled out Rai, a personalized AI wealth advisor and conversational chatbot. 

Rai runs on top of foundational models from OpenAI and Anthropic, and has been trained on the Range team's 275-plus years of combined financial advising experience. The tool automates the tasks of tracking client net worth, tax strategy, retirement plan, and account balances tracking and projection, among others. 

Range is also building specialized AI wealth advisors focused on various areas such as regulatory compliance, tax optimization, and investment guidance. The end goal is to deliver institutional-grade financial planning to investors.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Rate Cut Odds Tumble on Mixed Jobs Data
data.description:
The odds of an interest rate cut at the January 28 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting fell on Friday as investors digested a new jobs report. Traders are now pricing in a 5% chance that the Fed cuts rates by 25 bps, down from 11.1% a day ago.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During December, the unemployment rate was 4.4%, below the consensus estimate of 4.5%. November's unemployment rate was revised lower to 4.5% from 4.6%. In addition, wage growth rose by 3.8% year-over-year, above the estimate of 3.6%. A stronger labor market discourages rate cuts because job growth and higher wages can keep inflation elevated.

Rate Traders Look Past Weak Nonfarm Payrolls Data

However, it appears that rate traders are focusing on the positive aspects of the jobs report, as nonfarm payrolls increased by 50,000 in December, well below the estimate of 70,000. Furthermore, October and November's payrolls were revised lower by a combined 76,000 jobs.

For 2025, nonfarm payrolls rose by a total of 584,000, marking the worst year for job growth outside of a recession since 2003.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Tesla Rival Polestar Stock (PSNY) Drops despite 'Best Year' Sales in 2025
data.description:
Shares in Tesla's (TSLA) Swedish electric vehicle rival Polestar (PSNY) failed to get off the ground despite reporting its "best year ever" retail sales volume in 2025.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During the year, Polestar sold 60,119 cars, which is a 34% increase from a year ago, with sales boosted by its strategic focus on Europe, its core and largest market, instead of the U.S. and China, where the rivalry is cutthroat. The EV maker's retail sales for the last quarter of 2025 also rose 27% year-over-year, reaching 15,608 units.

Polestar Boosts Sales with Network Expansion

Michael Lohscheller, Polestar CEO, said the automaker achieved the result in spite of persistent external pressures and a tough market backdrop. "We are gaining market share and outselling many established car brands across key European markets," Lohscheller noted, crediting the growth to the 50% increase in the company's sales network and its "attractive" model lineup.

Polestar's vehicle lineup includes the luxury sport-utility vehicle (SUV), Polestar 3, and the smaller but sportier coupe-SUV, Polestar 4. The rise in its sales comes even as Tesla continues to lose ground in the region to Chinese rivals such as BYD (BYDDY) — the latter, for the first time, even beat Tesla to global battery electric vehicle sales in 2025.

Polestar's Losses Remain Source of Concern

However, investor reaction to Polestar suggests investors are still worried about the Swedish EV maker's mounting losses and debt profile amid U.S. tariffs and delays in model releases. In its third-quarter 2025 results released in November, the automaker's losses widened by 13% from a year ago to $365 million. Polestar also recently turned to a 1-for-3 reverse stock split to avoid being delisted on Nasdaq.

Meanwhile, the carmaker late last month raised $300 million in new financing, even as its backer Geely (GELYF) plans to convert about $300 million of debt into stock.

Are Polestar Shares a Good Buy?

On Wall Street, Polestar's shares have a Hold rating based on one rating issued in the last three months. This comes with no price target.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
